name: bad-blood
description: John Carreyrou's Bad Blood — extract investigative lessons from the Theranos scandal: how fraud happens in Silicon Valley, how charismatic founders deceive smart people, and how to spot red flags before it's too late
useCase: Helps entrepreneurs, investors, employees, and board members recognize fraud patterns, evaluate startup culture, understand whistleblower psychology, and practice rigorous due diligence — using the Theranos case as a master class in what NOT to do.
knownRisks: The book is a narrative account — the extracted patterns are Carreyrou's journalistic findings, not a peer-reviewed fraud detection framework
Applying these lessons requires judgment; not every charismatic founder and secretive startup is Theranos
